summary: Set 10 Session 1 (3-session set, first non-final session): Renamed `ai-router/` -> `ai_router/` so the package is directly Python-importable, and added a PEP 621 `pyproject.toml` at the repo root. After this session, `pip install -e .` from the repo root + `from ai_router import route` replaces the importlib.util.spec_from_file_location shim every adopter had to carry. Deliverables: git mv (40+ rename entries), `pyproject.toml` with [project.scripts] for the 7 CLI surfaces (close_session, report, reconciler, queue_status, heartbeat_status, restart_role, backfill_session_state) per the spec's literal naming, `__version__ = '0.1.0'` in __init__.py, `pytest.ini` testpath update, agent-instruction file rewrites (CLAUDE/AGENTS/GEMINI: file-map bullet, build/test, AI router import block), README adoption-section update (Step 1 mentions `ai_router/` + `pyproject.toml`; Step 3 is `pip install -e .`; Step 4 is `from ai_router import route`), workflow doc 'Importing the Router' section rewritten, planning docs path refs updated, session-set-authoring-guide.md tooling/infra example updated, scripts/verify_session_*.py (13 scripts) had the importlib shim replaced with direct `import ai_router` after sys.path.insert of repo root, tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/ TS/webview/README path refs updated (dist/ NOT rebuilt this session - Session 3 packages v0.12.0). Three docs/proposals/ files got an explicit Path note (2026-05-01) marking dashed-path body refs as audit-date snapshots. .gitignore updated for *.egg-info/, build/, dist/*.tar.gz/whl. Smoke test: fresh .venv + `pip install -e ".[tests]"` succeeded; import works; __version__ prints '0.1.0'. Tests: 676 passed under system Python (matches set 009 close baseline). Two tests under fresh .venv interpreter (`test_restart_role.py::TestRestartAgainstRealDaemon`) fail due to a pre-existing Windows venv-launcher PID redirector quirk (subprocess.Popen.pid != os.getpid() inside the spawned script); environmental, not a regression. Cross-provider verification: 3 rounds against gpt-5-4; final verdict VERIFIED with one non-blocking note about the Windows venv quirk above. Cumulative routed cost this session: $0.326 (verification only - no analysis routes per the standing operator cost-containment rule, which suspended Rule #17 routed authoring of ai-assignment.md for this set, same pattern set 009 used).

reason.specifics: Session 2 authors a GitHub Actions release workflow (OIDC trusted publishing) plus the per-release runbook; small-but-high-stakes prose+YAML work that benefits from Opus's careful-wording bar, mirroring this session's profile.
